RETROACTIVITY_CAVEAT_CRITICAL.consequence: For symbols with NO new split after 2025-12-30, the 2019-01-02..2025-12-30 overlap must REPRODUCE the sealed s21 DR9 values byte-for-byte (historical-reproduction check). For symbols WITH a new split, the historical overlap LEGITIMATELY re-scales (return-continuous) and MUST be documented (split-consistency audit, band 0.85-1.15). An UNDOCUMENTED divergence in the overlap = data-integrity failure -> HALT.
RETROACTIVITY_CAVEAT_CRITICAL.issue: split_only adjustment is RETROACTIVE: if any name split AFTER 2025-12-30, its entire pre-split history is re-divided by the new cumulative split factor. Therefore the refresh is NOT a simple append of new rows.
RETROACTIVITY_CAVEAT_CRITICAL.required_method: RE-FETCH FULL HISTORY (2019-01-02 -> as-of) per symbol and recompute split_only over the WHOLE series; then write the full refreshed series to the REFRESH dir.

authorization_phrase: Authorize s21 weekly RS paper data refresh RUN_BOOK only.
contingency_tree: Vendor/API failure or empty data for any symbol -> BLOCKER -> HALT (no partial write).
Undocumented overlap divergence (no-split symbol's history changed) -> DATA_INTEGRITY HALT (possible vendor drift); do NOT trust the refresh.
Documented new split detected -> recompute full-history split_only, document in manifest, run split-consistency check; pass -> OK, fail -> INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD.
Vendor not yet current (stale) -> HALT; retry after the target session settles.
downstream_coupling_note: The committed harness cycle (cycle.py) currently expects the filename suffix `_20190102_20251230.csv`. Pointing a forward cycle at the refreshed dir (new `_<ASOF>` suffix) will require either a small harness data-source/filename update (a SEPARATE BUILD authorization) or passing the explicit dir+pattern at the cycle RUN. This RUN_BOOK defines the DATA refresh only; wiring the cycle to the refreshed data is a separate downstream step.

per_symbol.overlap_check: byte/value match of 2019-01-02..2025-12-30 rows vs sealed s21 DR9 CSV (or documented split re-scale)

next_authorization_options.exact_phrase: Authorize s21 weekly RS paper data refresh fetch + verification workflow only — Tiingo split_only, frozen 48-name universe, FULL-HISTORY re-fetch + recompute, write to data/s21_weekly_rs_paper_refresh/raw (do NOT overwrite the sealed s21 DR9 CSVs), run SHA + calendar-alignment + split-consistency + historical-overlap-reproduction checks, HALT on stale/misaligned/undocumented-divergence; TIINGO_API_KEY from env only, never printed; no paper cycle, no broker, no live, no FRC.
next_authorization_options.title: Execute the data refresh fetch + verification
Defer / Pause s21 paper data refresh at RUN_BOOK.

output_directory.local_only: All output is LOCAL files; no upload, no network egress beyond the read-only Tiingo fetch at execution time.
output_directory.no_overwrite_rule: The refresh writes to a SEPARATE dir and MUST NOT modify, overwrite, or delete the sealed s21 DR9 CSVs (committed at d76c999). Sealed artifacts are immutable.

purpose: Bounded plan to bring the s21 paper-trading data current so a true FORWARD paper cycle (cycle 002+) can run. The committed harness (f73bd52) is broker-free and never fetches; it consumes operator-supplied LOCAL split_only CSVs. The sealed s21 DR9 CSVs end 2025-12-30, so a real forward cycle is blocked until refreshed data exists. This RUN_BOOK defines HOW to produce that refreshed data safely; it fetches nothing.

stale_data_kill_condition.action: HALT the refresh; do NOT write a partial/stale set as authoritative; do NOT run any paper cycle on stale data. The harness's own data-integrity guard would also NO-TRADE/kill on stale input. Report and retry the fetch later.
stale_data_kill_condition.definition: the refreshed data is STALE/invalid if the latest bar is OLDER than the target weekly-anchor session (vendor not current), OR any symbol returns empty/short history, OR calendar misalignment, OR an undocumented overlap divergence.
stale_data_kill_condition.freshness_assertion: last_date(refreshed) >= target weekly-anchor session; else STALE -> HALT.

target_date_range.end_approx_at_authoring: ~2026-05-29 (computed precisely at fetch; ~21-22 weekly anchors of new data)
target_date_range.end_rule: the most recent COMPLETED weekly-anchor session on or before the fetch run date
target_date_range.weekly_anchor_note: the weekly anchor (e.g., Friday-close signal) must be LOCKED before the first forward cycle (paper plan 3dd8b3c sec 1); the refresh fetches DAILY bars through the latest completed session and the cycle later selects the anchor bar

title: s21 weekly RS PAPER DATA REFRESH RUN_BOOK (sealed; framework ONLY; NO fetch yet) -- update the frozen 48-name split_only CSVs from 2025-12-31 through the latest completed weekly anchor; local-only; sealed DR9 set never overwritten
universe_policy: FROZEN -- byte-identical to the s21 sealed universe (DR9 d76c999 / SEAL ae11479). No add/drop/substitution. A different universe = a different candidate, not a data refresh.

vendor_and_convention.api_key_handling: TIINGO_API_KEY from environment ONLY at fetch time; never printed, logged, or committed
vendor_and_convention.implementation: Tiingo raw OHLCV x cumulative(1/splitFactor) for splits strictly AFTER each date; volume / cumulative factor; adjClose ignored; dividends NOT adjusted -- IDENTICAL to the s21 sealed convention

verification_checks.calendar_alignment: all 48 refreshed series share an IDENTICAL date vector through the as-of bar; the 2019-01-02..2025-12-30 portion must match the sealed 1759-bar NYSE calendar exactly, plus the new sessions; any misalignment -> HALT (no silent reindex).
verification_checks.historical_overlap_reproduction: for each no-new-split symbol, the overlap rows must equal the sealed s21 DR9 CSV values; mismatch without a documented split -> data-integrity HALT.
verification_checks.sha_verification: compute SHA256 of every refreshed CSV + the manifest; record in the manifest; re-verify on read.
verification_checks.split_consistency: for each documented split after 2025-12-30, verify return-continuity at the ex-date (close[ex]/close[prev] within band 0.85-1.15); fail -> INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD.
